name: The Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular from Quatretondeta
name_original: La Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular desde Quatretondeta
region_intro: Touring cycling around Quatretondeta offers routes through a landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and the presence of reservoirs. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often traversing hills and offering views of the surrounding sierras. This area provides a challenging environment for touring cyclists, with many routes climbing through rural and natural settings. Best touring cycling routes around Quatretondeta The most popular touring cycling route is Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This route features a loop around the scenic Guadalest Reservoir with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular from Quatretondeta, a difficult 22.5 miles (36.3 km) path. This route explores the Sierra de Almudaina, offering a challenging gravel experience with significant climbs. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Puig Campana – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Benasau, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes with views towards Puig Campana, often completed in about 4 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Quatretondeta is defined by mountainous ascents, reservoir views, and rural paths. The network offers options primarily for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 4 moderate and 19 difficult routes available. The routes in Quatretondeta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 1500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Quatretondeta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Quatretondeta?
A: There are 23 touring cycling routes around Quatretondeta, offering a variety of experiences through mountainous terrain and valleys. These routes are generally suited for moderate to difficult ability levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Quatretondeta?
A: The touring cycling routes in Quatretondeta are primarily designed for experienced cyclists. There are 4 moderate routes available, but no easy ones, indicating that the terrain often involves significant elevation changes and challenging conditions.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Quatretondeta?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952600227/guadalest-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views around the reservoir, or tackle &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952658099/the-sierra-de-almudaina-gravel-circular-from-quatretondeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Sierra de Almudaina – Gravel circular from Quatretondeta&lt;/a&gt; for a challenging gravel experience.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks can I expect to see on a touring cycling route?
A: The region offers numerous scenic points and natural landmarks. You might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Tudons&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3953285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confrides Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also provide views towards prominent features such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Campana&lt;/a&gt;, or natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Quatretondeta?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountainous ascents, the rewarding reservoir views, and the opportunity to explore rural paths away from traffic.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: While the routes primarily focus on natural landscapes, some, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31601115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Castle loop from Confrides&lt;/a&gt;, will bring you close to significant historical landmarks. Guadalest Castle itself is a notable attraction in the region, offering a glimpse into local history.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on touring cycling routes around Quatretondeta?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, touring cycling routes in Quatretondeta feature significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5301961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana – Ascent Puerto de Tudons loop from Benasau&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1470 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952658099/the-sierra-de-almudaina-gravel-circular-from-quatretondeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Almudaina gravel circular&lt;/a&gt; has over 1150 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes suitable for families with children?
A: Due to the prevalence of moderate to difficult routes with significant elevation, most touring cycling routes around Quatretondeta are not ideally suited for young children or families seeking very easy, flat rides. Experienced teenage cyclists might manage some of the moderate options.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Quatretondeta?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling, offering milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be very hot, and winters, while generally mild, can bring cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: Parking availability varies by starting point. Many routes begin in or near villages like Quatretondeta or Benasau, where street parking or small public car parks may be available. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Can I expect to find cafes or refreshment stops along the touring cycling routes?
A: While many routes traverse rural and natural settings, some pass through small villages where you might find local cafes or shops. For longer rides, it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, as refreshment stops may not be frequent, especially on more remote sections.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer views of reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by the presence of reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952600227/guadalest-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering continuous views of the scenic Guadalest Reservoir throughout the ride.
tips: Ruins in good condition located in a very beautiful environment. La Queirola is a farmhouse of Islamic origin of which there is already documentary evidence in the 13th century. After the conquest of the territory by the Conqueror King, it remained inhabited by 13 Moorish families who survived there in harsh conditions until their expulsion in 1602.
The climb to Loma Alta is magnificent but requires attitude and for the less skilled, a hike-a-bike.
